CREATE TABLE phrases (id INT PRIMARY KEY, phrase TEXT);
CREATE TABLE text_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, content TEXT);
INSERT INTO phrases VALUES (1, 'example phrase'), (2, 'test phrase'), (3, 'sample phrase');
INSERT INTO text_data VALUES (1, 'This is an example phrase in the text.'), (2, 'The test phrase is here.'), (3, 'No matches found.'), (4, 'Sample phrase is also present here.');
SELECT td.id, td.content, p.phrase FROM text_data td JOIN phrases p ON td.content LIKE '%' || p.phrase || '%' WHERE td.id IN (SELECT id FROM text_data WHERE content IS NOT NULL);
SELECT DISTINCT td.id, COUNT(p.id) AS phrase_count FROM text_data td LEFT JOIN phrases p ON td.content LIKE '%' || p.phrase || '%' GROUP BY td.id HAVING phrase_count > 0;
SELECT td.id, ARRAY_AGG(p.phrase) AS matched_phrases FROM text_data td JOIN phrases p ON td.content LIKE '%' || p.phrase || '%' GROUP BY td.id ORDER BY td.id;

WITH matched AS (SELECT td.id AS text_id, p.phrase FROM text_data td JOIN phrases p ON td.content LIKE '%' || p.phrase || '%') SELECT text_id, ARRAY_AGG(phrase) AS phrases_found FROM matched GROUP BY text_id;

SELECT td.id, td.content, ARRAY_AGG(p.phrase) AS found_phrases FROM text_data td LEFT JOIN phrases p ON td.content LIKE '%' || p.phrase || '%' WHERE p.phrase IS NOT NULL GROUP BY td.id HAVING FOUND_PHRAS = TRUE;
SELECT DISTINCT td.id, SUBSTRING(td.content FROM '[A-Za-z]+') AS extracted_words FROM text_data td WHERE td.content IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_occurrences FROM text_data td JOIN phrases p ON td.content LIKE '%' || p.phrase || '%';
